zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Edward Coleman於1456年7月1日出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德時，他的父親Richard Colman36歲，母親Lady Agnes Norreys36歲。他於1480年與Katherine Fulsnape在英國素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1505年3月5日在他的家鄉享年48歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Edward Coleman于1456年7月1日出生在英国英格兰素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德时，他的父亲Richard Colman36岁，母亲Lady Agnes Norreys36岁。他于1480年与Katherine Fulsnape在英国素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1505年3月5日在他的家乡享年48岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可蕾托 瓦爾迪費爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Coleman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Kalman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Gabor, Sandor, Imre, Lajos, Geza, Gyula, Katalin, Zoltan.

Hungarian (Kálmán): from the old Hungarian personal name Kálmán, meaning ‘remainder’ (from Turkic kal ‘to remain’), hence a protective name, which was given to infants to ward off evil and harmful spirits. In medieval times the name became particularly popular because it was confused with the German Christian name Koloman of Irish origin (see Kolman 2).

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Kalmen, an everyday form of Kloynemes (from Hebrew Kalonimos, which is from Greek kalos ‘lovely’ or kallos ‘beauty’ + onyma ‘name’). This Hebrew name is first recorded in the Talmud and has been used continuously since then. Among Hungarian Jews, it was sometimes confused with 1 above.

variants.name: Colman
meanings: Irish (Mayo and Sligo) and English: variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kohlmann   or   Kollmann  .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Clifford
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Collman
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German   Kollmann  , or of   Kohlmann  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eudard

meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Edward Coleman was born on 1 July 1456, in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Richard Colman, was 36 and his mother, Lady Agnes Norreys, was 36. He married Katherine Fulsnape in 1480, in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 5 March 1505, in his hometown, at the age of 48, and was buried in Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Edward Coleman est né le 1 juillet 1456, à Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Colman, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Norreys, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Katherine Fulsnape en 1480, à Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 5 mars 1505, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterré à Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Edward Colemanが1456年7月1日にイギリスイギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドで生まれた時，父Richard Colmanは36歳，母Lady Agnes Norreysは36歳であった。1480年にイギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドでKatherine Fulsnapeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1505年3月5日に故郷で48歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクリットル ワルディングフィールドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Edward Coleman(이)가 1456년 7월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Colman(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Agnes Norreys(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1480년 영국 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에서 Katherine Fulsnape(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1505년 3월 5일 그의 고향에서 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 리틀 월딩필드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Edward Coleman nasceu em 1 de julho de 1456, em Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Colman, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes Norreys, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Katherine Fulsnape em 1480, em Little Waldingfield, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Edward Coleman родился 1 июля 1456 г. в местности литл валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Richard Colman было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Norreys, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Katherine Fulsnape в 1480 г. в местности литл валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 5 марта 1505 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 48 лет и был похоронен в местности литл валдингфиелд, суффолк, Англия.
